page: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, featuring 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation and no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64pls_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and software compilation with Clang. It also exhibits high memory bandwidth for cached small-block reads, reaching 4383.1 GB/sec, and performs strongly in Redis SET operations at 80.12 million operations per second. However, single-core performance, text processing, and large-model LLM prompt processing represent relative performance bottlenecks, scoring in the average to weak categories.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64pls_v5 offers a high physical core density on the ARM64 architecture, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance for compute-heavy tasks. Recommended workloads include software compilation, multi-threaded web serving, Redis caching, and medium-scale LLM text generation, whereas memory-intensive databases or large-scale AI training are constrained by the memory capacity and lack of local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPLSv5 family, powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs with no hyperthreading, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, representing a compute-optimized 1:2 memory-to-core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and efficient software compilation, though single-core execution and text processing are average. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for scale-out, multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without requiring high memory density. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, Redis caching, and multi-threaded web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 128 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, optimized for compute-heavy scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 64 dedicated Ampere Altra cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM64 virtual machine with a compute-optimized memory ratio, designed for highly multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Features 128.0 GB of system memory with a compute-optimized 1:2 core-to-RAM ratio
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high cached memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage remote disks
Optimal for software compilation, Redis caching, and multi-threaded web serving
